articles.author: Lorian Belanger
articles.description: Iran, the United States and Israel reached a tentative, two-week ceasefire Wednesday in the war that tore across the Middle East and disrupted the global energy market, with U.S. President Donald Trump pulling back from his threats to destroy Iranian “civilization.”
articles.title: Live updates: US, Israel and Iran agree to a 2-week ceasefire

articles.author: Kareem Chehayeb, Abby Sewell
articles.description: Israeli strikes have hit central Beirut without warning, causing panic after the announcement of the ceasefire in he U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. Wednesday's strikes came after Israel said the ceasefire does not apply to its fight with the Iranian-backed Hezbollah. Israel's military called it the largest coordinated strike in the current conflict, hitting over 100 targets in Beirut and other areas. Lebanon’s health ministry said dozens are dead in an early estimate. Associated Press journalists saw charred bodies in cars and on the ground at one of the capital's busiest intersections. Hezbollah has not agreed to the ceasefire. Displaced families face uncertainty.
articles.title: Israeli strikes central Beirut after ceasefire in war with Iran

articles.author: Bassem Mroue, Jon Gambrell, Samy Magdy, Sam Metz
articles.description: Iran, the United States and Israel have agreed to a two-week ceasefire. The 11th-hour deal headed off U.S. President Donald Trump’s threat to unleash a bombing campaign that would destroy Iranian civilization. But hours after the announcement, Iran and Gulf Arab countries reported new attacks Wednesday. It was not clear if the strikes would scuttle the deal. U.S. Vice President JD Vance called it “fragile.” Even before the new attacks Wednesday, much about the agreement was unclear as the sides presented vastly different visions of the terms.
articles.title: US, Israel and Iran agree to a 2-week ceasefire but much remains unclear

articles.author: Aamer Madhani, Will Weissert, Josh Boak, Farnoush Amiri
articles.description: Over the course of a single day, President Donald Trump went from threatening Iran with “annihilation” to proclaiming that Iran's leadership had presented a “workable” plan that led him to agree to a two-week ceasefire. Trump says he expects this to pave the way to end the nearly six-week war with Iran. Trump's shift came as intermediaries led by Pakistan worked to head off a further escalation. Even China quietly pulled strings to urge Iran to find a path toward a ceasefire. Trump plans talks Wednesday with NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte, with the Strait of Hormuz expected to be at the center..
articles.title: How Trump moved from threats to a ceasefire with Iran

articles.description: Speaking at an event in Hungary, U.S. Vice President JD Vance called the ceasefire agreement “a fragile truce” but offered no details.
articles.title: Vance calls US-Iran ceasefire a 'fragile truce'

articles.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared victory Wednesday after the U.S. and Iran reached a 2-week ceasefire, praising Donald Trump as a "president of peace" who "chose mercy" in agreeing to the ceasefire.
articles.title: Hegseth says Trump 'chose mercy' in agreeing to 2-week ceasefire with Iran

articles.author: Scott Bauer
articles.description: Democratic-backed candidate Chris Taylor has won election to the Wisconsin Supreme Court. Her victory Tuesday grows the liberal majority on the court as cases affecting congressional redistricting, union rights and other hot button issues await. Taylor defeated Republican-backed Maria Lazar in the fourth straight victory for liberal court candidates dating back to 2020. Liberals are now guaranteed to hold a majority on the court until at least 2030. Spending and attention on this race were way down given that majority control was not at stake. Liberals will now have a 5-2 majority.
articles.title: Democratic-backed Chris Taylor wins Wisconsin Supreme Court race, growing liberal majority

articles.author: Joey Cappelletti
articles.description: Progressive Michigan Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed is teaming up with online streamer Hasan Piker for campus events that are already sparking backlash. Critics say Piker’s past remarks cross the line into hateful rhetoric. Supporters say Democrats need voices like his to reach younger voters, especially men who drifted right in 2024. In an interview with The Associated Press, Piker framed the reaction as part of a broader fight over the party’s direction. El-Sayed is leaning into that risk, betting that expanding the party’s reach — even through controversial figures — may be key to winning back voters.
articles.title: Michigan's Senate primary tests limits of Democrats' big tent

articles.author: Jeff Amy
articles.description: Republican Clay Fuller faces Democrat Shawn Harris in a special election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene in Congress. The winner of the election Tuesday would serve the rest of Greene's term. Harris led the first round on March 10, but Republicans still hold a clear advantage in the district. President Donald Trump endorsed Fuller, and Fuller says he backs Trump on every issue. Harris calls himself a practical “dirt-road Democrat” and says Fuller cannot put local needs first. The winner would need to run again in separate primaries and a general election later this year to keep the seat.
articles.title: Shawn Harris and Clay Fuller compete in Georgia to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene

articles.author: Michael R. Sisak
articles.description: A Long Island architect has pleaded guilty to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes known as the Gilgo Beach killings. Rex Heuermann entered the pleas on Wednesday in a courtroom packed with reporters, police and victims’ relatives. His decision brings finality to a case that bedeviled investigators, tantalized the public and spawned true-crime documentaries, podcasts and a Hollywood movie. Authorities say Heuermann killed the women over a 17-year span. Many of them were sex workers whose deaths received little attention until their remains were found buried together along an isolated beach highway. Heuermann faces life in prison and will be sentenced at a later date.
articles.title: Rex Heuermann to plead guilty in the Gilgo Beach killings
